# Current Bahamas — Compliance Checklist: Food & Beverage
# Version 1.0 | April 2026
# For: Restaurants, cafes, catering businesses, food trucks, bakeries
# Derived from master checklist — F&B-specific items added

---

## Client: ___________________________  Code: ________
## Business type: _____________  Seats / capacity: ____  Island: ________________
## Checklist prepared: _______________  By: ___________

---

## SECTION 1: BUSINESS LICENCE

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Business licence current — correct category (Restaurant / Café / Catering / Food Service) | [ ] | |
| Licence covers all service types (dine-in, takeaway, delivery, catering, pop-up) | [ ] | |
| Liquor licence current (if applicable) | [ ] | Separate from business licence |
| Renewal due date in Airtable | [ ] | |
| Financial statements ready if turnover >$250K | [ ] | |

**F&B note:** If the business serves alcohol, the liquor licence has its own renewal cycle. Track separately from the business licence.

---

## SECTION 2: VAT

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| VAT registered? | [ ] Yes [ ] No | |
| VAT applies to: food sales, beverage sales, catering contracts | [ ] | Confirm applicable treatment with accountant |
| Seasonal revenue patterns documented (high tourism season vs off-season) | [ ] | |
| Last VAT return current | [ ] | Period: ________ Filed: ________ |
| VAT filing calendar — all 12 periods in Airtable | [ ] | |

---

## SECTION 3: NIB

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| NIB employer number confirmed | [ ] | |
| Current employee count confirmed (kitchen, floor, bar staff) | [ ] | Count: ________ |
| Seasonal and part-time staff captured in NIB headcount | [ ] | Weekend / event staff |
| Last NIB payment current | [ ] | Period: ________ Paid: ________ |
| Any arrears outstanding | [ ] | |

---

## SECTION 4: TCC

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Current TCC on file | [ ] | |
| Any government catering contracts anticipated | [ ] | Government events, ministry catering |
| TCC expiry in Airtable — 30-day reminder active | [ ] | |

---

## SECTION 5: AGRICULTURE IMPORT PERMITS — CRITICAL FOR F&B

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Business imports any food products? | [ ] Yes [ ] No | Almost always Yes for restaurants |
| **KEY RULE: Permits must be obtained BEFORE goods are purchased** | [ ] Confirmed with manager | |
| Imported product categories identified: | | |
| — Fresh produce (fruit, vegetables) | [ ] | |
| — Dairy (cheese, butter, cream) | [ ] | |
| — Meat and poultry | [ ] | |
| — Seafood | [ ] | |
| — Packaged / processed food | [ ] | |
| — Seeds, plants, or agricultural inputs | [ ] | |
| Permit categories required for each product type identified | [ ] | |
| Permit lead times documented and aligned with ordering calendar | [ ] | Lead time varies by category |
| MyGateway Agriculture login confirmed | [ ] | |
| Current active permits on file | [ ] | |
| Ordering calendar reviewed against permit renewal dates | [ ] | |

**F&B alert:** This is the #1 compliance risk for restaurants. Buying produce before the permit is issued creates problems at the port. Build the permit calendar first, order second.

---

## SECTION 6: HEALTH AND FOOD SAFETY

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Ministry of Health food handler certificates current for all kitchen staff | [ ] | Note: CB tracks; refer to Ministry of Health for requirements |
| Food establishment inspection current | [ ] | |
| Certificate of Environmental Health compliance | [ ] | |

**Note:** Current Bahamas tracks these items but does not advise on health code compliance. Refer to Ministry of Health.

---

## SECTION 7: EXEMPTIONS

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| FIDEA eligibility (Family Island businesses) | [ ] | |
| Any planned equipment purchase: commercial kitchen, refrigeration, generators | [ ] | Trigger for exemption packaging |
| Existing concessions documented | [ ] | |
